criteria.label: State the kernel trick and write formulas for linear, polynomial, and RBF kernels.
criteria.example: Kernel trick: replace x·x′ with K(x,x′). Linear: K=x·x′. Poly: K=(γ x·x′+r)^d. RBF: K=exp(−γ‖x−x′‖²).

criteria.label: Fit linear and RBF SVMs on the same dataset and compare validation accuracy.
criteria.example: On sklearn make_moons, train SVC(kernel='linear') vs SVC(kernel='rbf', gamma='scale'); report which achieves higher 5-fold CV accuracy and why moons favors nonlinear K.

criteria.label: Build and inspect an RBF Gram matrix for three points.
criteria.example: For X=[[0,0],[1,0],[0,1]], compute rbf_kernel(X, gamma=1); verify symmetry, unit diagonal, and that off-diagonal K decreases as points move apart.

criteria.label: Explain implicit vs explicit feature maps with a degree-2 polynomial example.
criteria.example: For x∈ℝ², φ could include [x₁², x₂², x₁x₂, x₁, x₂, 1]; poly kernel degree 2 gives their dot product without constructing the 6D matrix.

criteria.label: Tune RBF γ and C with cross-validation and describe overfitting when γ is too large.
criteria.example: GridSearchCV on breast_cancer with gamma in [0.01,0.1,1] and C in [0.1,1,10]; note val score drops when γ=1 with n≈400 because boundaries memorize training islands.
